lks2.objective: Develop decision-making — choose between the safe 2-point run or the risky 6-point run-and-back.
successCriteria: Makes a conscious choice after each shot
Fielders agree their plan before the shot

uks2.objective: Refine risk-vs-reward decision-making — read fielder positions and ball direction before choosing 2 or 6.
successCriteria: Chooses the right shot type for the chosen run
Communicates the call clearly to teammates

equipment.item: Cones to mark the safe zone

space.description: A pitch with stumps at one end and a 'safe zone' marked some distance away. A hoop near the stumps for the fielders' ball return.

steps: Divide the class into small teams (max 4) — fielding and batting team.
Batters have 2 choices after each shot:
1. Run to the safe zone and arrive before the ball reaches the safe zone in the hands of a fielder (2 points).
2. Run to the safe zone AND back before the fielders can place the ball in the hoop (6 points).
Swap teams when every batter has had their 2 shots.
questions: Fielders, where is the best place to position yourselves?
What strategies can you use to keep yourself motivated if you get out or mishit the ball?
What factors help you decide whether to go for 2 or 6 points?

step.space: Increase or decrease the distance to the safe zone
step.task: Batters can use a tee to strike
step.equipment: Use different weights and shape balls

cues.phrase: "Decide BEFORE the ball — six or safe?"

errors.fix: Cap 'six attempts' per innings; reward smart 'safe' choices

errors.issue: Fielders bunching where last ball went
errors.cause: Following the ball, not covering gaps
errors.fix: Reset positions; cue 'cover the field, not the last ball'
